title: Glaziers, window fabricators and fitters
description: Install glass in windows, skylights, store fronts, and display cases, or on surfaces, such as building fronts, interior walls, ceilings, and tabletops. Grind, sand, or polish, using hand tools or hand-held power tools, a variety of metal, wood, stone, clay, plastic, or glass objects. Includes chippers, buffers, and finishers. Use hand tools or hand-held power tools to cut and trim a variety of manufactured items, such as carpet, fabric, stone, glass, or rubber. (Based on 7 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Read and interpret blueprints or specifications to determine the size, shape, color, type, or thickness of glass, location of framing, and installation procedures.

Assemble, erect, or dismantle scaffolds, rigging, or hoisting equipment; operate cranes or hoists with suction cups to lift large, heavy pieces of glass; and drive trucks to installation sites and unload mirrors or glass equipment.

Determine plumb of walls or ceilings using plumb lines and levels; measure and mark outlines or patterns on glass to indicate cutting lines; and measure mirrors and dimensions of areas to be covered to determine work procedures.

Prepare glass for cutting by resting it on rack edges or against cutting tables and brushing a thin layer of oil along cutting lines; score glass with cutters' wheels, breaking off excess glass by hand or with notched tools; and cut and remove broken glass prior to installing replacement glass.

Cut, fit, install, repair, or replace glass or glass substitutes in building interiors or exteriors or in furniture or other products; install pre-assembled metal or wood frameworks for windows or doors to be fitted with glass panels; and fasten glass panes into wood sashes or frames with clips, points, or moldings.

Grind or polish glass, smoothing edges when necessary; select files or other abrasives according to materials and finishes specified; and trim, scrape, or deburr objects or parts using chisels and other hand tools and equipment.

Pack spaces between moldings and glass with glazing compounds and trim excess material with glazing knives; cut and attach mounting strips, metal or wood moldings, rubber gaskets, or metal clips to surfaces in preparation for mirror installation; and secure mirrors in position using mastic cement, putty, bolts, or screws.
Select the type or color of glass or mirror according to specifications and confer with customers to determine project requirements or to provide cost estimates.

Create patterns on glass by etching, sandblasting, or painting designs; measure, cut, fit, and press anti-glare adhesive film to glass or spray glass with tinting solution to prevent light glare; and assemble and cement sections of stained glass together.

Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications; verify quality of finished workpieces by inspecting them, comparing them to templates, and measuring their dimensions; and remove completed workpieces from equipment or work tables.
Move furniture to clear work sites and cover floors or furnishings with drop cloths.
